summary: Documentation-only PR adding a new docs/standards.md review checklist for decomp PRs and worker output, covering source readability, matching tactics, data and naming rules, file hygiene, and verification expectations. The PR was closed with no merge timestamp in the loaded metadata.

key_files.role: new documentation file containing PR review QA checklist for decomp PRs and worker output
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Summary says add docs/standards.md as review checklist

what_changed.text: Added docs/standards.md with 256 lines of decomp PR review QA standards.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: +# Melee PR Review QA Standards

what_changed.text: Documented source rules for natural loops, typed fields over pointer math, recognizing header inlines, assert/report macros, literals, and canonical control flow/macros.

Documented matching tactic rules requiring targeted evidence for PAD_STACK/FORCE_PAD_STACK, declaration order changes, widened local lifetimes, dummy or volatile locals, manual inline expansion, direct global access, and temporary padded structs.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ### Require Evidence For Matching Tactics

what_changed.text: Documented rules to avoid new pragmas, register steering, and inline assembly except when tightly scoped and evidenced.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ### Avoid New Pragmas, `register`, And Inline Assembly

what_changed.text: Documented data, naming, file, formatting, and verification expectations, including respecting data sections and TU splits, conservative naming, truthful headers/includes, reviewable mechanical changes, and build/objdiff/checkdiff evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ## Data, Naming, And File Rules

smart_moves.text: Kept the PR documentation-only and reported that build/tests were not run because of the documentation-only scope.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Validation includes git diff --cached --check and Not run: build/tests (documentation-only change)

smart_moves.text: Grouped review guidance into explicit Do and Do not lists, making the checklist searchable and actionable for reviewers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: repeated Do:/Do not: subsections in docs/standards.md

decomp_lessons.lesson: Repeated generated-looking blocks should be checked against natural for/while/do-while forms when they differ only by index, pointer, or counter; exact int counters may matter for MWCC loop unrolling.
Prefer typed access through real fields or correct GroundVars/FighterVars/ItemVars arms over M2C_FIELD and raw pointer arithmetic when the type can be recovered.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ### Prefer Typed Fields Over Pointer Math

decomp_lessons.lesson: Expanded header asserts, especially jobj.h asserts, should be mapped back to identifiable HSD_JObj inline/helper forms rather than left as expanded __assert code.
Raw assert/report expansions should use project macros such as HSD_ASSERT, HSD_ASSERTMSG, HSD_ASSERTREPORT, OSReport, or OSPanic when they preserve the same source behavior.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ### Use Project Assert And Report Macros

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not promote literals into named data symbols without data ownership evidence from symbol metadata, section placement, or surrounding source.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ### Keep Literals Inline Unless Data Evidence Says Otherwise

decomp_lessons.lesson: Generated branch ladders, gotos, and repeated select-like assignments should be tested against ordinary structured C forms such as switch, ternary, ABS, MIN, MAX, and CLAMP.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ### Use Canonical Control Flow And Macros

decomp_lessons.lesson: Matching-only tactics should remain only with targeted local build, objdiff, or regression evidence and should not shift nearby functions, data order, or hide real types.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ### Require Evidence For Matching Tactics
raw_diff: intro says matching-only tactics should be backed by local build, objdiff, or regression evidence

decomp_lessons.lesson: Verification claims for matches should include narrow touched-object builds when possible and objdiff/checkdiff for every claimed matched symbol or unit.

naming_conventions.convention: Use semantic names only when the role is evidenced; keep fn_<addr>, lbl_<addr>, or module-address names when semantics are not proven.
Use short local role names such as fp, ip, gp, gobj, and jobj when those roles are clear.
Clean up generated temp_rXX-style names once the local role is known.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: For MWCC loop-shape problems, test exact int counters and preserve the original base pointer when later code needs it.

Keep PAD_STACK/FORCE_PAD_STACK, declaration-order steering, dummy or volatile locals, widened lifetimes, manual inline expansion, direct global access, and temporary padded structs only with targeted evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ### Require Evidence For Matching Tactics

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scope unavoidable pragmas tightly and prove pragma or inline-assembly exceptions do not affect adjacent functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ### Avoid New Pragmas, `register`, And Inline Assembly

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Inspect adjacent functions when changing pragmas, includes, data, literals, or TU ownership.

review_feedback.text: Build/tests were explicitly not run because the PR was documentation-only.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Validation - Not run: build/tests (documentation-only change)

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev[bot] reported for GALE01 that there were no changes.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: issue_comment by decomp-dev[bot] says Report for GALE01 (a8bc217 - ebefd62): No changes
